content.significance_en: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to cultivate patience, Free from any trace of animosity towards anyone at all, Since any potential source of harm is like a priceless treasure To the bodhisattva who is eager to enjoy a wealth of virtue.

visual.coincidence_meaning_en: Death: Snatches away lives; will take lives of others.

content.significance_en: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to strive with enthusiastic diligence— The source of all good qualities—when working for the sake of all who live; Seeing that even śrāvakas and pratyekabuddhas, who labour for themselves alone, Exert themselves as if urgently trying to extinguish fires upon their heads.

visual.coincidence_meaning_en: Death: Snatches away lives; will take lives of others.

content.significance_en: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to cultivate concentration, Which utterly transcends the four formless absorptions, In the knowledge that mental afflictions are overcome entirely Through penetrating insight suffused with stable calm.

visual.coincidence_meaning_en: Death: Snatches away lives; will take lives of others.

content.auspicious_day_short_desc_en: A day connected to wisdom energy and enlightened activity — suitable for mantra recitation and inner practice.
content.significance_en:
"The Day of the Dakini Festival"                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          Mother of all the victorious ones, Vajrayoginī,
Mother of diversity, Prajñāpāramitā,
Padma's consort, joyful, passionate and smiling—
Yeshe Tsogyal, homage to you!
content.notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab

visual.coincidence_meaning_en: Death: Snatches away lives; will take lives of others.

content.significance_en: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to cultivate wisdom, Beyond the three conceptual spheres, alongside skilful means, Since it is not possible to attain the perfect level of awakening Through the other five pāramitās alone, in wisdom’s absence.

visual.coincidence_meaning_en: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.

content.significance_en: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to study, reflect and meditate, Tirelessly, both day and night, without ever straying into idleness, In order to free oneself and others from this ocean of saṃsāra, Having gained this supreme vessel—a free, well-favoured human life, so difficult to find.

visual.coincidence_meaning_en: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings grief and sorrow.

content.significance_en: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to leave behind one’s homeland, Where our attachment to family and friends overwhelms us like a torrent, While our aversion towards enemies rages inside us like a blazing fire, And delusion’s darkness obscures what must be adopted and abandoned.

visual.coincidence_meaning_en: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings grief and sorrow.

content.auspicious_day_short_desc_en: A day to make offerings and prayers to Dharma Protectors for the removal of obstacles and protection of practice.
content.significance_en: Day dedicated to invoking Dharma Protectors like Mahakala and Palden Lhamo to remove obstacles and strengthen protection.
content.significance_bo:
"བོད་ཟླ་རེའི་ཚེས་ ༢༩ ཉིན་ནི་*ཆོས་སྐྱོང་སྲུང་མའི་དུས་ཆེན་ནམ་དུས་བཟང་*གལ་ཆེན་ཞིག་ཡིན། 
༡། གོ་དོན།
བོད་ཀྱི་ལོ་ཐོའི་ནང་ཟླ་རེའི་ཚེས་ ༢༩ ནི་*ཡི་དམ་དང་ཆོས་སྐྱོང་*རྣམས་ཀྱི་དུས་བཟང་དུ་བརྩི་བ་ཡིན། ལྷག་པར་དུ་ལོ་མཇུག་གི་དགུ་གཏོར་དང་། ཟླ་རེའི་ནང་གི་ནག་པོའི་ཕྱོགས་ཀྱི་མཐའ་མར་ཉེ་བའི་སྐབས་འདིར། དྲག་པོའི་ལས་སྒྲུབ་པ་དང་བར་ཆད་བཟློག་པའི་དུས་ཁྱད་པར་ཅན་ཞིག་ཏུ་ངོས་འཛིན་བྱེད།
༢། བྱེད་སྒོ།
ཉིན་འདིར་དགོན་སྡེ་ཁག་དང་ཁྱིམ་ཚང་སོ་སོའི་ནང་དུ།
 * གསོལ་ཁ་འབུལ་བ། ཆོས་སྐྱོང་སྲུང་མ་རྣམས་ལ་གཏོར་མ་དང་། གསེར་སྐྱེམས། བསངས་མཆོད་སོགས་རྒྱ་ཆེན་པོ་འབུལ་བ།
 * བསྐང་བཤགས་གཏོང་བ། རང་གི་དམ་ཚིག་ཉམས་ཆག་རྣམས་བཤགས་ཤིང་། སྲུང་མའི་ཐུགས་དམ་བསྐང་བའི་ཆོ་ག་བྱེད་པ།
༣། ཕན་ཡོན།
ཚེས་ ༢༩ ལ་སྲུང་མ་མཆོད་པའི་ཕན་ཡོན་ནི་བསམ་གྱིས་མི་ཁྱབ་པ་ཡོད་དེ།
 * བར་ཆད་ཞི་བ། ལམ་ལྷོངས་མ་ཡོང་བའི་གེགས་བར་དང་། མི་ཁ་ཁ་མཆུ། གནོད་པ་ཐམས་ཅད་ཞི་བར་བྱེད།
 * དམ་ཚིག་གཙང་མར་བཟོ་བ། བླ་མ་དང་ཆོས་སྐྱོང་དབར་གྱི་འགལ་འཁྲུལ་དང་དམ་ཚིག་ཉམས་པ་རྣམས་སོར་ཆུད་པར་བྱེད།
 * བྱ་བ་ལམ་འགྲོ། ཆོས་བཞིན་སྒྲུབ་པའི་ལས་དོན་ཐམས་ཅད་ལ་ཆོས་སྐྱོང་གིས་མགོན་སྐྱོབ་དང་ཕྱྭ་གཡང་སྩོལ་བར་བྱེད།
 * བསྟན་པ་སྲུང་བ། སྒེར་གྱི་དོན་ཙམ་མ་ཡིན་པར་སངས་རྒྱས་ཀྱི་བསྟན་པ་དང་འཛམ་གླིང་ཞི་བདེ་སྲུང་བའི་ནུས་པ་སྤེལ།
༤། དམིགས་བསལ་གྱི་དོན་སྙིང་།
སྲུང་མ་མཆོད་པ་ནི་ལྷ་གསོལ་བ་ཙམ་མ་ཡིན་པར། རང་གི་སེམས་ནང་གི་*དྲག་པོའི་ནུས་པ་*འཕེལ་བར་བྱས་ཏེ། ནང་གི་ཉོན་མོངས་པའི་དགྲ་བཅོམ་པ་དང་། ཕྱིའི་འཇིག་རྟེན་གྱི་བྱ་བ་གང་ཡང་བརྟན་པོར་སྐྱོང་བའི་སྙིང་སྟོབས་བསྐྱེད་པ་ལ་གོ་དགོས།
> ལམ་སྟོན། ཉིན་འདིར་ཁྱེད་ཀྱིས་རང་གི་ཡི་དམ་མམ་སྲུང་མའི་མ་ཎི་དང་སྙིང་པོ་བཟླས་པ་དང་། གཙང་བྲ་ལ་གཟབ་ནས་གསེར་སྐྱེམས་འབུལ་ན་ཤིན་ཏུ་ལེགས།"
content.notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab

visual.coincidence_meaning_en: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.

daily_restriction.raw: Avoid Funeral Rites or Merit Feasts

content.auspicious_day_short_desc_en: A day of introspection and renewal, traditionally used for purification practices and restoring commitments.
content.significance_en:
"The Day of the Hundred Sacred Peaceful and Wrathful Deities"

Confession of Negative Actions (Digpa Shagpa): As it is the final day of the month, it is a day for purifying and confessing the non-virtuous actions committed throughout that month.
Restoration and Purification (Sojong): It is also the day when the monastic community (Sangha) gathers to perform the "Sojong" ritual, a ceremony for restoring vows and purifying transgressions according to the Vinaya (monastic discipline).
content.notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab

visual.coincidence_meaning_en: Disharmonious: Splits relations; brings disputes.

content.significance_en: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to take to solitary places, Avoiding the unwholesome, so that destructive emotions gradually fade away, And, in the absence of distraction, virtuous practice naturally gains strength; Whilst, with awareness clearly focused, we gain conviction in the teachings.

visual.coincidence_meaning_en: Ample Food/Clothing: Good for giving generously.

content.significance_en: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to renounce this life’s concerns, For friends and relatives, long acquainted, must all go their separate ways; Wealth and prized possessions, painstakingly acquired, must all be left behind; And consciousness, the guest who lodges in the body, must in time depart.

visual.coincidence_meaning_en: Death: Snatches away lives; will take lives of others.

content.significance_en: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to avoid destructive friends, In whose company the three poisons of the mind grow stronger, And we engage less and less in study, reflection and meditation, So that love and compassion fade away until they are no more.

visual.coincidence_meaning_en: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings thieves and robbers.

content.significance_en: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to cherish spiritual friends, By regarding them as even more precious than one’s own body, Since they are the ones who will help to rid us of all our faults, And make our virtues grow ever greater just like the waxing moon.

visual.coincidence_meaning_en: Achievement: Fulfils wishes and brings blessings.

content.significance_en: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to take refuge in the Three Jewels, Since they will never fail to provide protection for all who call upon them, For whom are the ordinary gods of this world ever capable of helping, As long as they themselves are trapped within saṃsāra’s vicious cycle?

visual.coincidence_meaning_en: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

content.significance_en: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is never to commit a harmful act, Even though not to do so might put one’s very life at risk, For the Sage himself has taught how negative actions will ripen Into the manifold miseries of the lower realms, so difficult to endure.

visual.coincidence_meaning_en: Death: Snatches away lives; very negative coincidence.

content.significance_en: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to strive towards the goal, Which is the supreme state of changeless, everlasting liberation, Since all the happiness of the three realms lasts but a moment, And then is quickly gone, just like dewdrops on blades of grass.

visual.coincidence_meaning_en: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

content.auspicious_day_short_desc_en: A day dedicated to healing prayers, longevity practices, and the cultivation of compassion for those who are ill.
content.significance_en:
Bhagavan, whose compassion is equal toward all,
Just hearing your name clears the suffering of lower realms;
Medicine Buddha, healer of the diseases of the Three Poisons,
To you, the Light of Lapis Lazuli, I prostrate.
content.notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab

visual.coincidence_meaning_en: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and pain.

content.significance_en: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to arouse bodhicitta, So as to bring freedom to all sentient beings, infinite in number. For how can true happiness ever be found while our mothers, Who have cared for us throughout the ages, endure such pain?

visual.coincidence_meaning_en: Ambrosia: Strengthens healthy long life; good for business.

daily_restriction.raw: Avoid Funeral Rites or Merit Feasts

content.auspicious_day_short_desc_en: A sacred day to honor Guru Padmasambhava, strengthen guru devotion, and renew Vajrayana commitments.
content.significance_en:
Guru Dorjé Drolö
On the tenth day of the ox month, master and disciples confer,
And in the centre and on the borders of the land of snows you conceal millions of terma troves.
With these treasures you safeguard what remains of the teachings, all with your constant kindness
All-powerful Dorje Drolö, to you we pray.
Grant your blessing so that sudden misfortune, like untimely death, never befalls us,
And as soon as we transfer to the next life, we are born in the pure realm of Great Bliss,
And in the paradise of Lotus Light, in your very presence, Guru,
And become a source of limitless benefit to living beings.
content.notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab

visual.coincidence_meaning_en: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings thieves and robbers.

content.significance_en: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to make a genuine exchange Of one’s own happiness and well-being for all the sufferings of others. Since all misery comes from seeking happiness for oneself alone, Whilst perfect buddhahood is born from the wish for others’ good.

visual.coincidence_meaning_en: Burning: Generates suffering; brings war and pain.

content.significance_en: Even if others, in the grips of great desire, should steal, Or encourage others to take away, all the wealth that I possess, To dedicate to them entirely my body, possessions and all my merits From the past, present and future— this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.

visual.coincidence_meaning_en: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

content.significance_en: Even if others should seek to cut off my head, Though I’ve done them not the slightest wrong, To take upon myself, out of compassion, All the harms they have amassed—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.

visual.coincidence_meaning_en: Death: Snatches away lives; very negative coincidence.

content.significance_en: Even if others should declare before the world All manner of unpleasant things about me, To speak only of their qualities in return, With a mind that’s filled with love—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.

visual.coincidence_meaning_en: Disharmonious: Splits relations; brings disputes.

content.auspicious_day_short_desc_en: A powerful day for merit-making, purification, offerings, and strengthening positive intentions.
content.significance_en: "The Day of Amitabha Buddha"
content.notes: The Short description is for Auspicious Days tab

visual.coincidence_meaning_en: Ample Food/Clothing: Good for giving generously.

content.significance_en: Even if others should expose my hidden faults or deride me When speaking amidst great gatherings of many people, To conceive of them as spiritual friends and to bow Before them in respect—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.

visual.coincidence_meaning_en: Death: Snatches away lives; will take lives of others.

content.significance_en: Even if others whom I have cared for like children of my own Should turn upon me and treat me as an enemy, To regard them only with special fondness and affection, As a mother would her ailing child—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.

visual.coincidence_meaning_en: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings thieves and robbers.

content.significance_en: Even if others, equal or inferior to me in status, Should, out of arrogance, disparage me, To honour them, as I would my teacher, By bowing down my head before them—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.

visual.coincidence_meaning_en: Unsuitable: Exhausts prosperity; brings grief and sorrow.

content.significance_en: Even though I may be destitute and despised by all, Beset with terrible illness and plagued by evil spirits, Still to take upon myself all beings’ ills and harmful actions, Without ever losing heart—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.

visual.coincidence_meaning_en: Youthfulness: Brings lasting peace; good for celebrations.

daily_restriction.raw: Avoid Funeral Rites or Merit Feasts

content.significance_en: Even though I may be famous and revered by all, And as rich as Vaiśravaṇa, the god of wealth himself, To see the futility of all the glory and riches of this world And to remain without conceit—this is the practice of all the bodhisattvas.

visual.coincidence_meaning_en: Strength: Brings about auspiciousness; good for rituals.

content.significance_en: The practice of all the bodhisattvas is to subdue the mind With the forces of loving kindness and compassion. For unless the real adversary—my own anger—is defeated, Outer enemies, though I may conquer them, will continue to appear.

visual.coincidence_meaning_en: Death: Snatches away lives; will take lives of others.
